course_outcomes: Explain the meaning and importance of customs valuation
Describe the history, objectives, and structure of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement
Apply transaction value and identify when it may or may not be used
Explain Article 8 adjustments including commissions, containers, assists, royalties, proceeds, and optional adjustments
Use documentation properly in customs valuation analysis

units.title: Introduction to Customs Valuation
units.unit_goal: Understand what customs valuation is and why it matters in customs administration.

lessons.title: Meaning of Customs Value
lessons.goal: Understand the meaning of customs value.
micro_steps: Define customs value
Connect customs value to imported goods
Explain why customs value matters in declaration and assessment

lessons.title: Ad Valorem Duties
lessons.goal: Understand the relationship between customs value and ad valorem duty.
micro_steps: Define ad valorem duty
Explain how customs value becomes the duty base
Differentiate ad valorem thinking from specific-rate thinking

lessons.title: Why Valuation Rules Matter
lessons.goal: Understand why uniform customs valuation rules were needed.
micro_steps: Explain why different valuation systems caused problems
Explain why arbitrary customs values distort trade
Explain why traders need certainty

lessons.title: Foundation Match
lessons.goal: Match basic customs valuation ideas to their meanings.
micro_steps: Match customs value to duty basis
Match ad valorem to percentage-based duty
Match fair valuation to trade neutrality

lessons.title: Unit 1 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you understand the foundations of customs valuation.
micro_steps: Define customs value
Explain why it is important
Explain why consistent valuation rules are necessary

units.title: History and Objectives of Customs Valuation
units.unit_goal: Understand how the WTO customs valuation system developed and what it seeks to achieve.

lessons.title: Historical Development
lessons.goal: Understand the historical development of customs valuation systems.
micro_steps: Recognize earlier valuation systems such as the Brussels Definition of Value
Explain the Tokyo Round shift toward positive valuation rules
Explain the Uruguay Round and the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement

lessons.title: Article VII and WTO Context
lessons.goal: Understand the legal source of WTO customs valuation.
micro_steps: Identify Article VII as the legal foundation
Explain the connection between GATT and WTO valuation rules
Explain why customs valuation affects trade access

lessons.title: Objectives and Principles
lessons.goal: Understand the major objectives and general principles of the Agreement.
micro_steps: Explain fair, uniform, and neutral valuation
Explain why transaction value is preferred
Explain the need for simple and equitable criteria consistent with commercial practice

lessons.title: History and Objective Match
lessons.goal: Match historical stages and principles correctly.
micro_steps: Match Tokyo Round to price actually paid or payable
Match Uruguay Round to WTO valuation agreement
Match fairness and neutrality to the objectives of the Agreement

lessons.title: Unit 2 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate understanding of the history and objectives of customs valuation.
micro_steps: Explain how customs valuation rules developed
Explain the role of Article VII
Explain the main objectives of the Agreement

units.title: Structure of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement
units.unit_goal: Understand the parts, annexes, and core article groupings of the Agreement.

lessons.title: Main Parts of the Agreement
lessons.goal: Identify the main parts of the WTO Customs Valuation Agreement.
micro_steps: Identify what Part I covers
Identify what Part II covers
Identify what Part III and Part IV cover

lessons.title: Article Map
lessons.goal: Understand the major article groupings within the Agreement.
micro_steps: Identify Articles 1 to 8 as the valuation methods area
Identify Articles 9 to 17 as supporting rules
Identify Articles 18 to 24 as administration, treatment, and final provisions

lessons.title: Annexes and Notes
lessons.goal: Understand the role of the annexes and interpretative notes.
micro_steps: Explain Annex I
Explain Annex II
Explain Annex III and the place of interpretative notes

lessons.title: Structure Match
lessons.goal: Match parts and annexes to their functions.
micro_steps: Match Part I to valuation rules
Match Part II to administration and dispute settlement
Match Annex I to interpretative notes

lessons.title: Unit 3 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show that you understand the structure of the Agreement.
micro_steps: List the main parts
Explain the role of articles 1 to 8
Explain the role of the annexes

units.title: The Six Methods of Valuation
units.unit_goal: Learn the six valuation methods and the rule of sequential application.

lessons.title: Method 1 to Method 6
lessons.goal: Memorize the six customs valuation methods.
micro_steps: Name Method 1 to Method 6
Recognize transaction value as primary
Recognize fallback as the last resort

lessons.title: Sequential Application
lessons.goal: Understand that the methods are generally applied in order.
micro_steps: Explain the rule of sequential application
Explain why customs cannot skip methods arbitrarily
Recognize the importer’s option to request reversal of Articles 5 and 6

lessons.title: Method Order Match
lessons.goal: Place the valuation methods in the correct order.
micro_steps: Match Method 1 to transaction value
Place identical and similar goods correctly
Place deductive, computed, and fallback correctly

lessons.title: Methods Quiz
lessons.goal: Test your understanding of the six methods.
micro_steps: Identify the six methods
Identify the order
Recognize which method has primacy

lessons.title: Unit 4 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate mastery of the valuation methods and their sequence.
micro_steps: List all six methods in order
Explain why transaction value comes first
Explain the special reversal option for Articles 5 and 6

units.title: Transaction Value: Core Concept
units.unit_goal: Understand the transaction value method as the primary valuation method.

lessons.title: Meaning of Transaction Value
lessons.goal: Define transaction value accurately.
micro_steps: Define transaction value
Identify its basic components
Connect it to Article 8 adjustments

lessons.title: Three Key Elements
lessons.goal: Understand the three core elements needed for transaction value.
micro_steps: Identify the sale requirement
Identify the sale for export requirement
Identify the price actually paid or payable requirement

lessons.title: Why Transaction Value Is Preferred
lessons.goal: Understand why transaction value is the starting point.
micro_steps: Explain why transaction value is the primary method
Explain how it reflects the actual transaction
Explain why it best matches the value of the goods being valued

lessons.title: Transaction Value Match
lessons.goal: Match the elements of transaction value correctly.
micro_steps: Match sale to ownership transfer for a price
Match sale for export to the importing country
Match price actually paid or payable to total payment

lessons.title: Unit 5 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show that you understand the concept and structure of transaction value.
micro_steps: Define transaction value
Explain its three key elements
Explain why it is the preferred method

units.title: Transaction Value: Application
units.unit_goal: Apply transaction value in practical valuation situations.

lessons.title: Concept of Sale
lessons.goal: Understand what counts as a sale for customs valuation purposes.
micro_steps: Explain the commercial meaning of sale
Recognize that not all movements of goods involve a sale
Connect sale to transfer of ownership for a price

lessons.title: Situations That Are Not Usually Sales
lessons.goal: Recognize import situations where transaction value usually cannot be used because there is no sale.
micro_steps: Identify consignment as generally not a sale at importation
Identify branch transfers as possible non-sales
Identify leasing, loans, destruction imports, and barter complexities

lessons.title: Sale for Export
lessons.goal: Understand the requirement that the goods be sold for export to the country of importation.
micro_steps: Explain what sold for export means
Recognize that seller location may differ
Focus on the export purpose of the sale

lessons.title: Price Actually Paid or Payable
lessons.goal: Understand the price element in transaction value.
micro_steps: Explain price actually paid or payable
Recognize direct and indirect payments
Recognize discounts and credits
Understand split shipment implications

lessons.title: Application Scenario Lab
lessons.goal: Apply Article 1 to practical fact patterns.
micro_steps: Decide whether a sale exists
Decide whether the sale is for export
Identify whether the price element is acceptable

lessons.title: Unit 6 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate ability to apply transaction value to practical situations.
micro_steps: Identify whether a transaction qualifies as a sale
Explain sale for export
Explain price actually paid or payable in real examples

units.title: Restrictions, Conditions, Proceeds, and Related Parties
units.unit_goal: Understand the major Article 1 limits on use of transaction value.

lessons.title: Restrictions on Disposition or Use
lessons.goal: Understand what kinds of restrictions do and do not prevent use of transaction value.
micro_steps: Identify the permitted categories of restrictions
Explain the idea of substantial effect on value
Recognize the need for case-by-case assessment

lessons.title: Conditions and Considerations
lessons.goal: Understand conditions that may prevent transaction value from being used.
micro_steps: Explain what a problematic condition is
Recognize examples involving linked purchases or pricing based on other goods
Explain why non-determinable value causes difficulty

lessons.title: Proceeds and Related Parties
lessons.goal: Understand proceeds of subsequent resale and related-party influence.
micro_steps: Explain proceeds of subsequent resale
Explain why an Article 8 adjustment may be needed
Explain why related-party sales are tested by whether the relationship influenced the price

lessons.title: Related Persons Rules
lessons.goal: Learn the main legal categories of related persons.
micro_steps: Identify legal categories of related persons
Recognize direct and indirect control
Recall the 5 percent voting stock test

lessons.title: Restriction and Relationship Match
lessons.goal: Sort transaction value limits into the correct categories.
micro_steps: Match restriction types correctly
Match condition examples correctly
Match related-party criteria correctly

lessons.title: Unit 7 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you understand when Article 1 may fail or need closer examination.
micro_steps: Explain restrictions and conditions
Explain proceeds of subsequent resale
Explain related-party influence on price

units.title: Article 8 Adjustments: General Rules
units.unit_goal: Understand the overall logic of Article 8 adjustments.

lessons.title: What Article 8 Does
lessons.goal: Understand the role of Article 8 in customs valuation.
micro_steps: Explain why Article 8 is read together with Article 1
Explain that adjustments are added when criteria are met
Explain why additions cannot be made twice

lessons.title: General Conditions for Additions
lessons.goal: Understand when an adjustment may lawfully be made.
micro_steps: Explain that the cost must be incurred by the buyer
Explain that it must not already be included
Explain objective and quantifiable data

lessons.title: No Unlisted Additions
lessons.goal: Understand the closed nature of Article 8 additions.
micro_steps: Explain that only Article 8 additions may be made
Explain why customs cannot add costs outside Article 8
Connect this rule to valuation certainty

lessons.title: Adjustment Rule Match
lessons.goal: Match the three general requirements for lawful additions.
micro_steps: Match buyer-incurred costs
Match not already included
Match objective and quantifiable data

lessons.title: Unit 8 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate control over the general rules for Article 8 additions.
micro_steps: Explain the purpose of Article 8
List the three conditions for additions
Explain why additions outside Article 8 are not allowed

units.title: Article 8 Adjustments: Commissions, Containers, and Assists
units.unit_goal: Understand the first group of compulsory Article 8 additions.

lessons.title: Commissions and Brokerage
lessons.goal: Understand buying commissions, selling commissions, and brokerage.
micro_steps: Differentiate buying and selling commissions
Explain why buying commissions are excluded
Explain brokerage and why customs must examine the facts carefully

lessons.title: Containers and Packaging
lessons.goal: Understand the treatment of container and packing costs.
micro_steps: Explain what counts as a container under Article 8
Explain the nature of packing costs
Identify examples of retail and export packing

lessons.title: Assists
lessons.goal: Understand the concept and categories of assists.
micro_steps: Define assists
List the categories of assists
Explain why assists must be apportioned reasonably
Explain how imported and domestically produced assists can differ in treatment

lessons.title: Assist Apportionment
lessons.goal: Understand how the value of assists is measured and apportioned.
micro_steps: Explain how prior use affects assist value
Explain repair or modification costs
Explain how leased or developed assists may be valued
Explain documentation for apportionment

lessons.title: Compulsory Additions Match
lessons.goal: Sort Article 8.1(a) and 8.1(b) items correctly.
micro_steps: Match buying commission to exclusion
Match selling commission to inclusion
Match containers and packing to addition
Match assists to addition

lessons.title: Unit 9 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you understand commissions, containers, and assists in customs valuation.
micro_steps: Explain buying vs selling commission
Explain containers and packing
Explain assists and their apportionment

units.title: Article 8 Adjustments: Royalties, Proceeds, and Optional Adjustments
units.unit_goal: Understand the more complex Article 8 additions and the role of transport terms.

lessons.title: Royalties and Licence Fees
lessons.goal: Understand when royalties and licence fees form part of customs value.
micro_steps: Explain the three-part test for royalties
Recognize intellectual property examples
Differentiate royalties that relate to the goods from those that do not

lessons.title: Rights to Reproduce and Distribute
lessons.goal: Understand the special treatment of reproduction and distribution rights.
micro_steps: Explain why reproduction rights in the importing country are usually excluded
Explain when distribution rights may still be added
Connect both issues to the condition-of-sale test

lessons.title: Proceeds of Subsequent Resale
lessons.goal: Understand proceeds under Article 8.1(d).
micro_steps: Define proceeds of subsequent resale
Differentiate proceeds from unrelated financial payments
Explain why Article 8 adjustment may be required

lessons.title: Optional Adjustments and Incoterms
lessons.goal: Understand Article 8.2 optional adjustments and the role of delivery terms.
micro_steps: List optional adjustments under Article 8.2
Explain why FOB and CIF matter for customs valuation
Explain treatment of transport, insurance, and storage costs

lessons.title: Advanced Adjustment Scenario Lab
lessons.goal: Apply advanced Article 8 rules to practical situations.
micro_steps: Decide whether a royalty is dutiable
Decide whether a later payment is a proceed
Use Incoterms to decide whether freight or insurance is already included

lessons.title: Unit 10 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate understanding of royalties, proceeds, and optional adjustments.
micro_steps: Explain the royalty test
Explain proceeds of subsequent resale
Explain optional adjustments and the role of delivery terms

units.title: Documentation in Customs Valuation
units.unit_goal: Understand the role of documents in establishing and verifying customs value.

lessons.title: Importance of Documents
lessons.goal: Understand why documentation matters in customs valuation.
micro_steps: Define document and document check
Explain why documents matter for customs valuation
Explain wider customs functions supported by documents

lessons.title: Core International Trade Documents
lessons.goal: Identify the key documents used in customs valuation work.
micro_steps: Identify the most important valuation documents
Explain what information each document typically contains
Connect documents to the elements of transaction value

lessons.title: Reading Documents for Valuation
lessons.goal: Understand what customs looks for in supporting documents.
micro_steps: Identify key information fields used in valuation analysis
Explain why payment terms and Incoterms matter
Explain how customs cross-checks documents

lessons.title: Document Match
lessons.goal: Match documents to the valuation facts they help establish.
micro_steps: Match commercial invoice to price and sale details
Match bill of lading to shipment details
Match packing list to quantity and packaging details
Match sales contract or purchase order to contractual conditions

lessons.title: Unit 11 Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Show you understand how documents support customs valuation.
micro_steps: Explain why documents matter
Identify key trade documents
Explain how documents support transaction value verification

units.title: Grand Mastery Path
units.unit_goal: Bring the entire Customs Valuation I topic together for revision and exam readiness.

lessons.title: Method and Agreement Quiz
lessons.goal: Review the Agreement structure and the six valuation methods.
micro_steps: Recall the parts of the Agreement
Recall the six methods
Recall the rule of sequence

lessons.title: Transaction Value Quiz
lessons.goal: Review Article 1 and the key limits on Method 1.
micro_steps: Apply Article 1 concepts
Recognize when Method 1 may fail
Recall related-party and proceeds issues

lessons.title: Article 8 Mixed Review
lessons.goal: Review compulsory and optional adjustments under Article 8.
micro_steps: Identify compulsory additions
Identify advanced additions
Apply objective and quantifiable data reasoning

lessons.title: Integrated Valuation Scenario Lab
lessons.goal: Analyze a full customs valuation scenario from start to finish.
micro_steps: Determine whether Method 1 applies
Identify relevant Article 8 additions or exclusions
Use documents to support the valuation decision

lessons.title: Grand Checkpoint
lessons.goal: Demonstrate complete understanding of Customs Valuation I.
micro_steps: Explain customs valuation foundations
Explain the structure of the Agreement
Explain transaction value and its limits
Explain Article 8 adjustments
Explain the role of documentation

lessons.title: Legendary Review Path
lessons.goal: Final mixed review for long-term retention and exam readiness.
micro_steps: Mixed concept questions
Mixed transaction value questions
Mixed Article 8 questions
Mixed documentation questions

daily_practice.description: Recycles weak lessons, commonly confused Article 1 conditions, related-party rules, and Article 8 adjustment categories.
